mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, marked by a sharp rise in the unemployment rate, signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, validating corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power, with the interaction term p-value exceeding 0.10 over a 5-year sample, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports near-term earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during tightening periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 in walk-forward tests restricted to tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal fails to predict returns during inflationary shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and corporate profitability to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction effect is statistically insignificant or reverses sign in walk-forward tests, or positive returns occur in < 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects the underlying trajectory of aggregate economic activity, where higher growth rates sustain corporate earnings expansion and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently near zero across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during recessionary phases.
failure_condition: SPX rallies in > 70% of consecutive industrial production decline months, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary regimes.
